title: Peppery Crispy Brazilian Beans Stew
description: A comforting, peppery stew of black beans simmered with aromatic spices and topped with a crispy chickpea and cashew garnish for an extra crunch.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add diced onion and garlic, sauté until fragrant and translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in diced red bell pepper, tomato paste, and smoked paprika, cooking for another 2 minutes.
Add the black beans, canned diced tomatoes with their juices, vegetable broth, soy sauce, cumin, coriander, black pepper, and star anise. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and let the stew simmer for 15 minutes to meld flavors.
While the stew simmers, toast the chickpeas in a dry skillet over medium heat with 1 tsp olive oil, salt, and a pinch of cayenne until golden and crisp, about 5-7 minutes.
In a small bowl, toss the cashews with 1 tsp olive oil and a pinch of salt. Toast in the skillet until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
Once the stew is hot and the flavors are well blended, remove the star anise and adjust seasoning with salt if needed.
Serve the stew in bowls, topping each serving with toasted chickpeas and cashews for a satisfying crunch.
